title: Small R/Python client to query the published catalog
context: To lower reuse friction, a small R/Python client lets researchers query the published CC-BY catalog (read-only) without re-implementing parsing. The client only reads published artifacts; it adds no data. Standing guardrails bind this task: open-access sources only (no dbGaP/EGA/biobank/controlled-access); COSMIC/OncoKB are link-only; donor fields are published-only with no re-identification or external linkage; every assertion carries field-level provenance; the deliverable is de-identified research metadata, never patient data and never a clinical/therapeutic recommendation.
objective: Build a small R/Python client that queries the published CC-BY catalog, with tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Client reads the published CC-BY catalog (JSON/CSV) and supports lookup by RRID/DepMap ID/Cell Model Passports ID and basic filtering.
Client only consumes published artifacts; it embeds no data and no credentials.
Ships tests/examples against a fixture catalog; code MIT-licensed; CI green.
resources: ICLAC Register of Misidentified Cell Lines
output: clients/ R and/or Python client for the published catalog, green in CI (PR).
